behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module-to-client handoff: request builder + API adapter wiring. A unit test of a helper string builder could miss dropped fields at the integration seam. REAL-INFRA is not required (capturing payload via in-process fake client is enough). Rejected System because behavior text only requires request content, not real network/subprocess/OS behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no dependence on model judgment or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the classifier module to a fake API client that records outbound payload. Call classify() with a taxonomy and assert the sent request contains every valid label exactly as configured.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on response handling across modules (API response parsing + taxonomy validation + returned value). In-process fake API response can trigger the real failure mode. Rejected Unit because testing only the validator function may miss parser/adapter mapping issues. Rejected System because no real network behavior is needed. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because output quality from a real model is not required; a deterministic fake invalid label is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client returning an out-of-taxonomy label (e.g., "medical"). Invoke classify() and assert returned value is "unknown" and never the raw unexpected label.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text is explicitly about normalization determinism for identical inputs; this is one local function/object boundary with no I/O. Rejected Integration because no module handoff is required to expose this failure; direct function calls catch it. Rejected higher levels since no real infra or model call is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization repeatedly with the same raw response fixtures (case/whitespace/format variants already fixed) and assert the exact same normalized label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live AI API calls and consistency of model-provided classification. A mock would hide real nondeterministic/model-behavior failures. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification, not a multi-stage agent pipeline with sequential model decisions. Rejected System as minimum because correctness depends on model output behavior, not only transport/network mechanics.
behaviors.test_description: Run N repeated real provider calls for the same document/taxonomy, record raw provider outputs and final labels, and assert label changes occur only when raw provider response changed.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at boundary between token-budget logic and dispatch path (policy decision must actually gate outgoing call). In-process tokenizer/config + fake client can catch real failure. Rejected Unit because isolated policy-function tests can miss that over-budget requests still get dispatched. Rejected System because no real OS/network behavior is needed. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no dependence on model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a low token budget, pass an over-budget document, and assert module applies configured policy (truncate or reject) and only dispatches compliant requests to fake client.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: To catch real failure, test must use the real AI provider’s reported usage; stubs hide provider-side tokenization/accounting differences. Behavior execution passes through a real AI API call, so deterministic assertions still require agentic-level infrastructure per LLM-DEP rule. Rejected System as minimum because assertion depends on model API’s actual usage semantics, not generic HTTP mechanics. Rejected Workflow because only one agentic step per call.
behaviors.test_description: Send representative documents to the real provider through the module and assert provider-reported token usage metadata is <= configured budget for each call.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is retry orchestration around API errors; deterministic fake client can return rate-limit signal then success to expose failure. Rejected System because real network/rate-limit conditions are not required to validate wait-and-retry control flow. Rejected Agentic because no dependence on model output quality/content. Rejected Workflow because this is not a multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client scripted to return rate-limit error then success; assert module delays per policy, retries, and returns successful classification instead of surfacing immediate error.
